summary: Kendra Wallace, CTO of Brightpath Commerce, contacted Aegis Cloud Security support regarding a $1,400 discrepancy on her February invoice. Marcus Williams identified an overage charge of 2.31 terabytes under the Aegis Protect module, which was traced to a backup policy change made on January 3rd that added five new workloads including database instances. The team clarified that billing is calculated on pre-compression raw data, meaning the policy expansion unintentionally pushed usage over the 10 TB contracted tier. Marcus agreed to document the findings in a case summary and connect Kendra with account manager Devon Park to discuss a potential billing adjustment.
actionItems: Marcus Williams: Send case summary email to Kendra including invoice reference INV-2026-0847, January usage figures, January 3rd policy change log, and Devon Park's contact information
Kendra Wallace: Reach out to account manager Devon Park to discuss potential billing adjustment for the overage charge
Sarah Chen: Ensure Kendra's infrastructure lead is looped in to enable usage threshold alerts in the Aegis Protect portal dashboard
Kendra Wallace: Have infrastructure lead review usage dashboard in the portal and configure real-time usage alerts going forward

keyMoments.text: Kendra identifies the unexpected data retention overage charge of 2.3 TB on the invoice, expressing surprise given their consistent data footprint
keyMoments.speaker: Kendra Wallace

keyMoments.text: Marcus discovers a backup policy change on January 3rd adding five new workloads, explaining the root cause of the overage
keyMoments.speaker: Marcus Williams

keyMoments.text: Kendra questions whether anything can be done about the charge given it was an unintentional configuration consequence rather than deliberate overuse
keyMoments.speaker: Kendra Wallace

keyMoments.text: Sarah recommends enabling usage threshold alerts in the portal to proactively notify the team before hitting tier limits in the future
keyMoments.speaker: Sarah Chen
